name: Dimensional Homogeneity
description: The principle that you can only add/subtract quantities with the same units

content.overview: You can't add apples and oranges. You also can't add meters and seconds! Dimensional homogeneity is the rule that says: only add/subtract quantities with the same dimensions. {{viz:diagram-01}}
content.explanation:
This is super simple but really important. {{viz:diagram-02}}

**The Rule: Both sides of an equation must have the same dimensions (units).**

Also: You can only add or subtract quantities if they have the same dimensions.

**What does this mean?**

You can do: 5 meters + 3 meters = 8 meters ✓ (both are length)
You can do: 10 seconds + 5 seconds = 15 seconds ✓ (both are time)

You CANNOT do: 5 meters + 3 seconds = ??? ✗ (different dimensions!)
You CANNOT do: 10 kg + 5 m/s = ??? ✗ (mass + velocity makes no sense)

**Why is this a LAW?**

Because physics equations describe reality, and reality is consistent. A distance is always a distance. A time is always a time. They can't magically become the same thing.

If you write an equation like: distance = speed + time

That's saying: [L] = [L T⁻¹] + [T]

The left side is length. The right side is (velocity) + (time), which doesn't even make sense dimensionally! This equation is WRONG. {{viz:chart-01}}

**In equations:**

In the equation: **distance = speed × time**

Left side: distance = [L]
Right side: speed × time = [L T⁻¹] × [T] = [L]

Dimensions match! ✓ {{viz:anim-01}}

**In addition/subtraction:**

You CAN write: **Total distance = distance₁ + distance₂ + distance₃**

Because all distances have the same dimension [L].

But you CANNOT write: **Total = distance + speed + mass**

Because [L] ≠ [L T⁻¹] ≠ [M]. They're different dimensions.

**Simple Rule for Checking Your Work:**

1. Look at your equation
2. Write the dimensions of each term
3. Check that all terms on the left have the same dimension
4. Check that all terms on the right have the same dimension
5. Check that left = right dimension
6. If any don't match, your equation is WRONG
keyPoints: Only add/subtract quantities with the SAME dimensions
Both sides of an equation must have the SAME dimensions
You can't add meters + seconds, or kg + m/s
Use it to check if your equations are right
content.whyItMatters: This is your final check on whether an equation makes physical sense. If dimensions don't match, something is definitely wrong. Use it to catch mistakes!
